description_long: This is a small, single-story wooden cabin elevated on short concrete piers, featuring a deep green gable roof with a rectangular skylight. The front facade has a covered porch with a set of stairs leading up to it, supported by simple posts and bordered by a railing. The exterior walls are dark brown, punctuated by several rectangular windows with blue-tinted glass and yellow trim, arranged asymmetrically along the sides. The roof overhangs the porch, providing shelter and shade, and the porch itself is open, allowing for outdoor seating or entry. The cabin's structure suggests a cozy, utilitarian design suitable for residential or recreational use in an urban or park setting. The materials and proportions evoke a rustic yet functional architectural style.
description: A small, dark brown wooden cabin with a green gable roof, front porch, and asymmetrical windows, elevated on concrete piers.
description_view_0: Front view showing the covered porch, central stairs, and main entrance beneath the overhanging green gable roof.
description_view_1: Side view displaying two rectangular windows with yellow trim and the roof skylight, as well as the porch extending from the left.
description_view_2: Rear view with a plain brown wall, no visible windows or doors, and the green gable roof with skylight.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a single window and a long, mostly unbroken wall, plus the porch visible at the far end.
attribute_roof_type: gable roof with moderate pitch, deep overhangs, and a single rectangular skylight; covered in green shingles
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical arrangement with two small rectangular windows on one side and a single window on the opposite side, all with yellow trim and blue glass; no windows on the rear
attribute_door_placement: single exterior door centered on the front facade, accessed via the porch and stairs
